events.title: US Warships Clear Mines in Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: Two US Navy warships transited the Strait of Hormuz to commence clearing Iranian-laid mines, a move that Iran's Revolutionary Guards threatened to respond to severely. This marks the first such transit since the conflict began, as President Trump stated the US is actively clearing the waterway.

events.title: US and Iran Fail to Reach Peace Deal in Pakistan Talks
events.desc: High-level talks between the US and Iran in Pakistan concluded without an agreement. US Vice President JD Vance stated that Iran did not accept the US's 'final and best offer,' while Iranian officials suggested the US was seeking an excuse to end the negotiations. The talks aimed to end the six-week conflict.

events.title: Israeli Strikes Hit Gaza Police Checkpoint
events.desc: An Israeli airstrike targeted a police checkpoint in Bureij camp in the central Gaza Strip, resulting in at least six fatalities and several injuries. The Israeli military has not yet commented, but has previously targeted Hamas-led police forces.

events.title: Lebanon Strikes Amidst Fragile Ceasefire
events.desc: Despite a US-Iran ceasefire announcement, Israel launched a significant wave of strikes across Lebanon, hitting areas including the southern suburbs of Beirut and southern Lebanon, killing at least 182 people and wounding hundreds. Iran's deputy foreign minister called these strikes a 'grave violation' of the ceasefire.

events.title: Iranian Counterair Campaign Hits US Air Bases
events.desc: Iranian drones and missiles struck Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia, destroying an E-3 Sentry and damaging multiple KC-135 tankers. This follows earlier attacks on the same base, marking a significant success for Iran's asymmetric counterair strategy against US forces in the region.
events.location: Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia

events.title: Iran Imposes Internet Blackout Amidst Strikes
events.desc: Following strikes by adversaries on military installations, the Iranian government deliberately throttled internet traffic by 98%, causing a near-total blackout. This action was aimed at the Iranian populace, hindering communication and information flow within the country during a period of external attacks.

events.title: US Accuses Iran of Minelaying in Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: President Trump stated that US forces are 'clearing' the Strait of Hormuz and that all 28 of Iran's minelaying ships have been sunk. This claim was made as US warships began clearing mines in the strait, a move Iran's Revolutionary Guards vowed to counter forcefully.

events.title: Israeli Settlements Approved in Occupied West Bank
events.desc: Israel has approved the establishment of 34 new settlements in the occupied West Bank, leading to apprehension among Palestinians about potential land loss. This move follows a period of heightened tensions and conflict in the region.

events.title: Two Waves of Bombing at Iranian School Compound
events.desc: New videos verified by Bellingcat show multiple missile impacts on an IRGC compound and an adjacent school in Minab, Iran. The footage reveals two distinct waves of strikes, providing further detail on the deadly attack.

events.title: US Deploys Mines Over Iranian Village
events.desc: Evidence suggests the US deployed Gator Scatterable Mines over the village of Kafari near Shiraz, Iran, resulting in civilian casualties. Experts identified the munitions as US-made Gator anti-tank mines, indicating a potential escalation in tactics against Iranian territory.

events.title: French Nuclear Deterrence Doctrine Shift
events.desc: French President Macron announced a new 'forward deterrence' doctrine, offering to deploy French Strategic Air Forces to allied European countries. This move aims to enhance deterrence by spreading nuclear assets more widely across the continent.

events.title: Hungarian Government Passwords Exposed Online
events.desc: Approximately 800 Hungarian government email addresses and passwords have been leaked online, compromising security protocols across 12 ministries. The exposed passwords, some including terms like 'Snoopy' and 'Adolf,' raise concerns about sensitive data security.
